title: nymphoides cordata
intro: Nymphoides cordata, also known as the blue water lily or water bluebells, is a floating aquatic plant native to East Asia and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Nymphoides cordata include its potential to reduce blood sugar levels, lower cholesterol, and protect against cardiovascular disease. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
